title: At the Restaurant
description: Ordering food, splitting the bill and talking to waiters

rows.label: Restaurant
rows.english: Restaurant — smaller local places are often called pohostinstvo (pub-restaurant); a kaviareň is a café

rows.label: Menu
rows.english: Menu / Food menu. Jedálny = food/dining, lístok = list/ticket. A nápojový lístok is the drinks menu

rows.label: Waiter
rows.english: Waiter (male). Say 'Prepáčte!' or 'Čašník, prosím!' to get attention

rows.label: Waitress
rows.english: Waitress (female) — the form you'll most often hear in Slovak restaurants

rows.label: Table
rows.english: Table. 'Voľný stôl' = free table. 'Rezervovaný stôl' = reserved table

rows.label: Reservation
rows.english: Reservation / Booking. 'Mám rezerváciu' = I have a reservation

rows.label: Starter
rows.english: Starter / Appetiser (pred = before, jedlo = food)

rows.label: Main course
rows.english: Main course (hlavné = main, jedlo = dish/food)

rows.label: Dessert
rows.english: Dessert. Popular choices: palacinka (crêpe), zmrzlina (ice cream), štrúdľa (strudel)

rows.label: Drink
rows.english: Drink / Beverage. The drinks menu is the nápojový lístok

rows.label: Bill
rows.english: Bill / Check. In Slovakia the waiter will NOT bring the bill automatically — you must ask: 'Môžem dostať účet, prosím?'

rows.label: Tip
rows.english: Tip / Gratuity (literally 'for drinks money'). ~10% is customary. Say 'Nechajte si zvyšok' = Keep the change

rows.label: Ask for menu
rows.english: Can I see the menu?

rows.label: Recommend
rows.english: What do you recommend? (formal plural — the polite way to address a waiter)

rows.label: Order
rows.english: I'll have... (the standard ordering phrase — literally 'I'll give myself')

rows.label: Would like
rows.english: I would like... (male: chcel, female: chcela — polite alternative to Dám si)

rows.label: Allergic
rows.english: I'm allergic to... (male: alergický, female: alergická). E.g. Som alergická na orechy = I'm allergic to nuts

rows.label: Get the bill
rows.english: Can I get the bill please? Always ask — the bill never comes automatically in Slovakia

rows.label: Split the bill
rows.english: Can we split the bill? (rozdeliť = to divide/split)

rows.label: Together or separate
rows.english: Are we paying together or separately? (the waiter will often ask this first)

rows.label: Keep the change
rows.english: Keep the change. (zvyšok = remainder/change)

rows.label: Dine in or take away
rows.english: Dine in or take away? (tu = here, so sebou = with you / to go)

rows.label: National dish
rows.english: Potato dumplings with bryndza sheep cheese and bacon bits — Slovakia's national dish. A must-try!

rows.label: Cabbage soup
rows.english: Sauerkraut soup with sausage or smoked meat — a Slovak staple, especially at Christmas and celebrations

rows.label: Goulash
rows.english: Goulash — hearty beef and paprika stew, typically served with bread dumplings (knedľa)

rows.label: Schnitzel
rows.english: Breaded schnitzel (vyprážaný = fried/breaded, rezeň = cutlet). Often pork (bravčový) or chicken (kurací)

rows.label: Beer
rows.english: Beer — excellent local brands: Zlatý Bažant, Šariš, Corgoň. Served with a thick foam head (pena)

rows.label: Wine
rows.english: Wine — Slovakia has quality wine regions in the south and east. Červené = red, Biele = white, Ružové = rosé

rows.label: Mineral water
rows.english: Mineral water (colloquial for minerálna voda). Sýtená = sparkling, Nesýtená = still

rows.label: Coffee
rows.english: Coffee — espresso style. Malá čierna = small black, Veľká čierna = large black, Biela = with milk

words.english: National dish — potato dumplings with sheep cheese
National dish — potato dumplings with sheep cheese
Can I get the bill please?

scenarios.situation: You arrive at a restaurant and the host asks 'Máte rezerváciu?' What are they asking?
choices: Do you have a loyalty card?
Would you like a table for two?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Máte rezerváciu? = Do you have a reservation? Rezervácia = reservation/booking. In popular Slovak restaurants, especially on weekends, it's worth calling ahead — say 'Chcem si rezervovať stôl na dve osoby' (I'd like to reserve a table for two).

scenarios.situation: The waiter at a fast food counter says 'Tu alebo so sebou?' What are the two options?
choices: Dine in or pay by card
scenarios.answerMeaning: Tu = here (dine in), So sebou = with you (take away / to go). You'll hear 'Tu alebo so sebou?' at fast food places, bakeries, and casual restaurants. Reply 'Tu, prosím' (here please) or 'So sebou, prosím' (to go please).

scenarios.situation: You want to order Slovakia's national dish. What do you ask for?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Bryndzové halušky = potato dumplings with bryndza (Slovak sheep cheese), topped with bacon bits. It's Slovakia's national dish and a must-try! You'll find it on most traditional Slovak restaurant menus.

scenarios.situation: Your meal was great and you want to leave a tip. The bill is €20, you pay €22. What do you say?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Nechajte si zvyšok = Keep the change. Zvyšok = remainder/change. Tipping in Slovakia is customary (usually 10%) — simply round up or say 'Nechajte si zvyšok' after paying. If paying by card, you can add a tip amount verbally before the transaction.

scenarios.situation: You're dining with 3 friends and everyone wants to pay their own share. What do you tell the waiter?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Môžeme si rozdeliť účet? = Can we split the bill? Rozdeliť = to split/divide. Alternatively say 'Každý platí sám' (everyone pays for themselves) or ask for separate bills: 'Štyri separátne účty, prosím' (four separate bills please).

scenarios.situation: You have a nut allergy. Which phrase do you say to the waiter before ordering?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Som alergický/á na orechy = I'm allergic to nuts. Alergický (male) / Alergická (female). Always declare food allergies — Slovak kitchen staff take them seriously. Common allergens in Slovak food: orechy (nuts), mlieko (milk), múka/lepok (flour/gluten), vajcia (eggs).

scenarios.situation: The waiter comes to your table and says 'Čo si dáte?' What are they asking?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Čo si dáte? = What will you have? / What would you like to order? Dať si = to have (when ordering). The standard exchange: waiter asks 'Čo si dáte?' and you reply 'Dám si...' (I'll have...). Very natural and common in Slovak restaurants!

scenarios.situation: You finish your meal. The bill hasn't arrived. What do you say to the waiter?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Môžem dostať účet, prosím? = Can I get the bill please? The bill never comes automatically in Slovakia — you always need to ask. A quick 'Účet, prosím!' or the universal 'writing' hand gesture also works perfectly.

items.sentence: Dám si ___ halušky — je to slovenské národné jedlo.
items.translation: I'll have ___ halušky — it's Slovakia's national dish.
Can I get the ___ please? I want to pay.

items.sentence: Čo ___? — Dám si guláš a pivo, prosím.
items.translation: What ___? — I'll have goulash and beer please.
Is there a free ___ here? There are four of us.

items.sentence: Aký ___ by ste si dali? — Dám si víno, prosím.
items.translation: What ___ would you like? — I'll have wine please.

items.sentence: ___ nám priniesla jedálny lístok a vysvetlila špeciality dňa.
items.translation: The ___ brought us the menu and explained the daily specials.

items.sentence: Dám si ___ halušky — je to slovenské národné jedlo.
Čo ___? — Dám si guláš a pivo, prosím.
Aký ___ by ste si dali? — Dám si víno, prosím.
___ nám priniesla jedálny lístok a vysvetlila špeciality dňa.

exercises.scenario: 🍽️ You are at a restaurant in Bratislava for dinner. The waiter takes your order.

turns.snailMessageEnglish: Good evening! Welcome. Do you have a reservation?
turns.snailReply: Výborne! Váš stôl je pripravený. Môžem vám priniesť jedálny lístok?
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Excellent! Your table is ready. Can I bring you the menu?

choices.english: Yes please. And what do you recommend?

turns.snailReply: Odporúčam bryndzové halušky — je to naše špeciálne jedlo!
turns.snailReplyEnglish: I recommend bryndzové halušky — it's our special dish!

choices.slovak: Dám si bryndzové halušky a minerálku, prosím.
choices.english: I'll have bryndzové halušky and a mineral water please.

turns.snailReply: Výborná voľba! A niečo na pitie okrem minerálky?
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Excellent choice! And something else to drink besides mineral water?

choices.slovak: Dám si aj pohár vína, prosím.
choices.english: I'll also have a glass of wine please.

turns.snailReply: Dobre. Vaše jedlo bude o chvíľu. Príjemné posedenie!
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Good. Your food will be ready shortly. Enjoy your time!

choices.slovak: Ďakujem! Bolo to výborné. Môžem dostať účet, prosím?
choices.english: Thank you! It was excellent. Can I get the bill please?

turns.snailReply: Ráčim! Tu je váš účet. Platíte spolu alebo zvlášť?
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Of course! Here is your bill. Are you paying together or separately?

choices.english: Together please. I'm paying by card.

turns.snailReply: Dobre. Spolu je to dvadsaťdva eur. Prosím priložte kartu.
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Good. The total is twenty-two euros. Please tap your card.

choices.slovak: Hotovo. Nechajte si zvyšok — bolo to skvelé!
choices.english: Done. Keep the change — it was great!

turns.snailReply: Ďakujem veľmi pekne! Príjemný večer a tešíme sa na vás opäť!
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Thank you very much! Have a lovely evening and we hope to see you again!

exercises.scenario: 🍽️ Tentoraz len po slovensky! Objednajte si večeru v reštaurácii.

turns.snailReply: Výborne! Váš stôl je pripravený. Môžem vám priniesť jedálny lístok?
Odporúčam bryndzové halušky — je to naše špeciálne jedlo!

choices.slovak: Dám si bryndzové halušky a minerálku, prosím.

choices.slovak: Dám si aj pohár vína, prosím.

turns.snailReply: Dobre. Vaše jedlo bude o chvíľu. Príjemné posedenie!

choices.slovak: Ďakujem! Bolo to výborné. Môžem dostať účet, prosím?

turns.snailReply: Ráčim! Tu je váš účet. Platíte spolu alebo zvlášť?
Dobre. Spolu je to dvadsaťdva eur. Prosím priložte kartu.

choices.slovak: Hotovo. Nechajte si zvyšok — bolo to skvelé!

turns.snailReply: Ďakujem veľmi pekne! Príjemný večer a tešíme sa na vás opäť!
